message:
fix: sort releases by published_at to avoid stale nightly tag (#8666) (#8674)

GitHub's /releases endpoint sorts by created_at of the release API
object, not by published_at. When a release is re-published or a draft
is later promoted, the most recent nightly tag may not be at position 0.

Fetch 10 recent releases instead of 1, filter to non-draft nightly
tags, and sort by published_at descending so the Nightly Release Pulse
card always shows the correct latest tag.

Fix applied to both the Go backend handler and the Netlify Function.

Fixes #8666

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
